mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which boosts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of quarters, or the interaction term loses explanatory power when added to a baseline macro model.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and triggers positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods following industrial production acceleration, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a suppressed federal funds rate compresses real yields and forces markets to price in aggressive future tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following payroll growth, or the rolling information coefficient remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, which triggers corporate earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >50% of confirmed recession quarters, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial output and decreases in consumer inflation indicate a supply-side boom that lowers production costs and boosts profit margins, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in out-of-sample tests.
